title: World War II
description: This exercise will address the causes and consequences of World War II as well as compare methods of war through the lens of the theme GOV. The two types will be causes and consequences of World War II and comparing methods of war. Each type will have a model and will scale 5 items, for a total of 12.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments led to the rise of fascist authoritarian leadership in Germany in the 1930s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: legislation passed in parliament
a military coup
revolution of the working class
anti-colonial sentiment in parliament

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following political factors contributed to both World War I and World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of nationalism
assassination attempts
a robust alliance system
anti-colonial rebellions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are characteristics of fascism in the 1930s?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of national unity, violence, and propaganda
populism, democratic structures, and a demagogue leader
the power of the proletariat, class consciousness, and government control of resources
collective ownership, an emphasis on equality, and a strong central government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following was the most crucial factor behind Germany’s readiness to embrace Nazism in the 1930s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hitler promised to end the humiliation and economic suffering brought on by the effects of World War I.
People who practiced Judaism generally lived separately from mainstream society.
The Treaty of Versailles blamed Germany for World War I but allowed for widespread military industrialization.
Poor working conditions and widespread poverty led to class consciousness and worker solidarity.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following could a historian use as evidence that World War I and World War II were the same conflict?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Japan expanded and gained more colonies until the end of World War II.
Germany continued to build its empire into the middle of the 1900s.
The United States used economically aggressive policies that spread the Great Depression to Europe.
Multinational corporations based in Allied nations competed with German companies for control of colonial resources.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Fascism attacks the whole complex [system] of democratic ideologies and rejects them ... Fascism denies that the majority, through the mere fact of being a majority, can rule human societies; it denies that this majority can govern by means of a periodical consultation; it affirms the ... inequality of men, who cannot be levelled by such a mechanical and extrinsic fact as universal suffrage…

Source: Benito Mussolini, “The Doctrine of Fascism” 1932, Italy.

**The excerpt above most reflects which of the following aspects of fascism?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rejection of democracy
equality for all citizens
political violence
class consciousness

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Bugs Bunny: Come on and get ’em folks, come on, step right up and get ’em.
Any bonds today? Bonds of freedom, that’s what I’m selling
Any bonds today? 
Scrape up the most you can!
Here comes the freedom man, asking you to buy a share of freedom today 
Naw, many stamps today!
Give, kiddies! We’ll be blessed, we all invest in the U.S.A.!

Source: Irving Berlin, animated song, “Any Bonds Today?” United States, 1942. 

**Which of the following examples of mobilization across countries in World War II is most similar to the above excerpt from the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Canada rationed food and ramped up production in order to support the British front lines.
Soviet Russian households consumed less in order to fund a larger military.
The government in the United States increased funding for war technology like nuclear weapons.
The United Kingdom recruited individuals in India to fight in the war.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Bugs Bunny: Come on and get ’em folks, come on, step right up and get ’em.
Any bonds today? Bonds of freedom, that’s what I’m selling
Any bonds today? 
Scrape up the most you can!
Here comes the freedom man, asking you to buy a share of freedom today 
Naw, many stamps today!
Give, kiddies! We’ll be blessed, we all invest in the U.S.A.!

Source: Irving Berlin, animated song, “Any Bonds Today?” United States, 1942. 

**Which of the following examples of mobilization across countries during World War I is most different from the excerpt above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Households in Soviet Russia had to use less food and fuel.
Families in the United States planted victory gardens to ration their food.
British women moved to rural areas in order to provide more food to the front lines.
The German government appealed to patriotism and duty to make workers save resources and work in industrial arms factories.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following examples are most similar to that which is depicted in the image above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A poster that shows a woman in an apron holding a bunch of jars.
options.caption: United States of America: Office for Emergency Management. Office of War Information. Domestic Operations Branch. Bureau of Special Services. Between 1941 and 1945. Public domain.

choices.content: The British government sent many women into the countryside to increase food production.
British colonial production increased in order to provide much-needed provisions.
Soviet households consumed less food in order to reserve resources for the front lines.
The Japanese invaded Korea and later China in order to gain more resources.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best summarizes the advantage totalitarian states had in mobilizing for World War II?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The state already controlled industrial production as well as food and fuel distribution.
Totalitarian states could force their citizens to join the military.
Technological and scientific discoveries moved faster in totalitarian nations.
People living in totalitarian states enjoyed higher standards of living.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following claims would a historian use to support the argument that the Americans made a correct decision in using nuclear weapons at the end of World War II?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The use of the bomb prevented America from having to invade Japan on land.
Nuclear technology could be a more humane way to conduct war.
In the long run, more lives would be saved by creating a system of mutually assured destruction to prevent future wars.
While the bomb would be destructive, it would prolong the war since Japan would never surrender.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>...Immediately after that, the blast came. I was bare from the waist up, and the blast was so intense, it felt like hundreds of needles were stabbing me all at once. The blast grew large holes in the walls of the first and second floor...Near the Miyuki Bridge, there was a police box. Most of the victims who had gathered there were junior high school girls from the Hiroshima Girls Business School and the Hiroshima Junior High School No.1. 
>
They had been mobilized to evacuate buildings and were outside when the bomb fell. Having been directly exposed to the heat rays, they were covered with blisters, the size of balls, on their backs, their faces, their shoulders and their arms. The blisters were starting to burst open and their skin hung down like rugs. Some of the children even have burns on the soles of their feet. They’d lost their shoes and run barefoot through the burning fire.

Source: Yoshito Matsushige, photojournalist, Account of the Hiroshima Bombing, 1945, Japan.

**The evidence from the excerpt above best supports which of the following claims?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nuclear weapons are a threat to humanity and the environment and should be destroyed.
The use of nuclear weapons was necessary to end the war.
Dropping nuclear bombs on Japan deterred other countries from attacking the United States.
While the bomb was destructive, there were more survivors than in communities that had been fire-bombed like Dresden and Tokyo.
